creator: eugen@freebsd.org
text:
It is a pity the PR was neglected. Partially because it does not specify FreeBSD version, I'm sure.

It does not specify if the system uses NAT to process traffic for clients. In standard setup, NAT processing is performed in interrupt contents and this explains why multiple NAT sessions increase CPU usage and impair locking performance.

Close this 9 years old PR. Feel free to re-open it if the problem is still relevant.
